verbGlossary.OVERSEES: {source} of a subordinate {target} work, reviewing output and making resource or escalation decisions
verbGlossary.ESCALATES TO: {source}issues, budget overruns, or schedule risks to a higher-authority {target} for decision or intervention
verbGlossary.DELEGATES TO: Assigns specific scope areas, tasks, or administrative responsibilities to a subordinate {target} while retaining accountability
verbGlossary.REPORTS TO: Provides regular status updates, field data, and issue flags to a supervisory {target} in the management chain
verbGlossary.PARTNERS WITH: Works as a co-equal peer on project execution, with one {target} leading office functions and the other leading field operations
verbGlossary.ADVISES: Provides expert analysis, recommendations, or field intelligence to a decision-making {target} without holding final authority
verbGlossary.DIRECTS: Issues day-to-day work instructions, sequences tasks, and assigns resources for a subordinate {target} to execute
verbGlossary.COORDINATES: {source}plans of multiple trade crews or subordinate {target}s to prevent conflicts and maintain production flow
verbGlossary.ASSIGNS WORK TO: {source}scheduled activities into specific daily tasks and distributes them to individual craft workers or crews
verbGlossary.MENTORS: Provides on-the-job training, skill development, and career guidance to a less experienced {target} in the field or office
verbGlossary.ASSISTS: Performs supporting tasks under the direct supervision of a more experienced {target} as part of formal trade training
verbGlossary.SUPERVISES: {source}of a technical or administrative {target}, reviewing deliverables and providing direction
verbGlossary.SUPPORTS: Provides technical assistance, data, or administrative services that enable another {target} to perform their primary function
verbGlossary.BRIDGES TO: {source}link between office-based management and field operations, translating between both contexts
verbGlossary.AUDITS: {source}reviews of another {target} safety or quality compliance, documenting findings and requiring corrective action
verbGlossary.TRAINS: Delivers formal or toolbox-talk safety and quality training to field personnel to maintain regulatory compliance and awareness
verbGlossary.INSPECTS: Performs field inspections of installed work or site conditions under another {target} area of responsibility
verbGlossary.HOLDS ACCOUNTABLE: Requires a field {target} to correct non-conforming work or unsafe conditions before proceeding with the next phase
verbGlossary.RETAINS: {source} and contracts with {target} or firm to perform services on the owner's behalf for the duration of the project
verbGlossary.AUTHORIZES: {source} for {target} to proceed with procurement, scope changes, expenditures, or other controlled actions
verbGlossary.APPROVES FOR: {source} and signs off on design decisions, value engineering proposals, or scope changes on behalf of the owner
verbGlossary.TRANSITIONS TO: {source}project leadership, institutional knowledge, and open-item logs from one phase-specific {target} to the next
verbGlossary.HANDS OFF TO: {source}defined scope of work or data package to a downstream {target} at a phase boundary
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONS: {source} {target} to produce a specific deliverable such as a baseline schedule, cost forecast, or analysis
verbGlossary.RELIES ON: Depends on another {target} specialized output (cost reports, schedule analysis, safety metrics) as input for decision-making
verbGlossary.RECONCILES WITH: Cross-checks financial data between two {target}s to ensure cost tracking, invoicing, and reporting are aligned and accurate
verbGlossary.COORDINATES WITH: {source} with a peer {target} across functional boundaries to align deliverables, data, or processes
verbGlossary.ENABLES: Provides the technology infrastructure, tools, or platform access that another {target} requires to perform their function
verbGlossary.VALIDATES FOR: {source}verifies that work completed by another {target} meets design intent, specifications, or performance criteria
verbGlossary.FEEDS: {source} to a higher-level staffing or coverage metric
verbGlossary.CONTRIBUTES TO: Provides a component measurement that rolls up into a broader project-level coverage or staffing indicator
